title: Face processing EEG dataset for EEGLAB
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 18 participants performing a face processing task involving perceptual judgments of famous, unfamiliar, and scrambled faces. The data were simultaneously acquired with MEG and structural MRI. EEG data have been repackaged in EEGLAB format following HED annotation and preprocessing including channel extraction, fiducial addition, event correction, resampling to 250 Hz, and run merging. The dataset supports investigation of neural responses to face categorization and repetition effects across two sessions. Note: MEG and EEG were recorded simultaneously in one session; structural MRI scans were acquired in a separate session and are preserved to support source localization.
methods_description: EEG data were simultaneously recorded with MEG during a perceptual task in which 18 participants viewed photographs of faces (famous, unfamiliar, scrambled) and made symmetry judgments via keyboard response. Each face was presented twice with either immediate or delayed (5-15 presentations) repetition. Following event log augmentation and HED (v8.0.0) annotation, data were processed to extract EEG channels from MEG/EEG recordings, add fiducials, rename EOG and EKG channels, extract and correct events (34 ms latency shift), resample to 250 Hz, and merge runs 1-6. Data were saved in EEGLAB .set format. Channel locations should be imported from BIDS electrodes.tsv files.
